cves.name: Dirty COW Copy-on-Write Race Condition
cves.description: Race condition in mm/gup.c in the Linux kernel allows local users to gain privileges by leveraging incorrect handling of a copy-on-write (COW) feature to write to a read-only memory mapping.

cves.name: Dirty Pipe Arbitrary File Overwrite
cves.description: A flaw was found in the way the flags member of the new pipe buffer structure was lacking proper initialization in copy_page_to_iter_pipe and push_pipe functions, allowing an unprivileged local user to write to pages in the page cache backed by read-only files.

cves.name: PwnKit Polkit pkexec Local Privilege Escalation
cves.description: A local privilege escalation vulnerability in polkit's pkexec component. Out-of-bounds read in argument parsing allows any unprivileged user to gain root privileges on a default installation.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade polkit to patched version. Remove SUID bit from pkexec as workaround.

cves.name: nf_tables Use-After-Free Local Privilege Escalation
cves.description: A use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel's netfilter nf_tables component. The nft_verdict_init() function allows positive values as a drop error within the hook verdict, leading to a double-free when NF_DROP is issued with a drop error resembling NF_ACCEPT.

cves.name: Netfilter nf_tables Use-After-Free (Anonymous Sets)
cves.description: Use-after-free in Netfilter nf_tables when processing batch requests. An unprivileged local user with CAP_NET_ADMIN in any user namespace can achieve root privileges.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.4-rc1 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: GameOver(lay) Ubuntu OverlayFS Privilege Escalation
cves.description: On Ubuntu, unprivileged users may use OverlayFS to set privileged extended attributes on the upper files without appropriate security checks. Affects Ubuntu-specific kernel patches.

cves.name: StackRot Maple Tree Use-After-Free
cves.description: A use-after-free flaw was found in the handling of maple tree nodes in the Linux kernel's memory management. A local attacker can use this to escalate privileges.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.4.1, 6.1.37, or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: Route4 Classifier Use-After-Free
cves.description: Use-after-free vulnerability in net/sched/cls_route.c (route4 classifier). Allows local privilege escalation from unprivileged namespaces on default configurations.

cves.name: Filesystem Context Integer Underflow
cves.description: Integer underflow in the legacy_parse_param function in fs/fs_context.c. Allows container escape and local privilege escalation with CAP_SYS_ADMIN in user namespace.

cves.name: Netfilter x_tables Out-of-Bounds Write
cves.description: A heap out-of-bounds write in net/netfilter/x_tables.c allows local privilege escalation. Originally discovered as a 15-year-old bug in setsockopt IPT_SO_SET_REPLACE.

cves.name: Baron Samedit Sudo Heap Buffer Overflow
cves.description: Heap-based buffer overflow in sudo before 1.9.5p2. When sudo runs a command in shell mode (sudoedit -s), any local user can escalate to root.

cves.name: Netfilter nf_tables Use-After-Free (NFT_SET_ELEM_CATCHALL)
cves.description: Use-after-free in net/netfilter/nf_tables_api.c when processing NFT_MSG_NEWSET with NFT_SET_ELEM_CATCHALL flag. Local user can escalate to root.

cves.name: OverlayFS SUID File Privilege Escalation
cves.description: A flaw was found in the Linux kernel where unauthorized access to the execution of the setuid file with capabilities was found in the OverlayFS subsystem.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.2-rc6 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: Sequoia seq_file Size-to-Int Overflow
cves.description: An out-of-bounds write in the filesystem layer via seq_file. By creating, mounting, and deleting a deep directory structure, a local attacker can write out-of-bounds to memory and escalate privileges.

cves.name: Netfilter nft_fwd_dup Heap Buffer Overflow
cves.description: Heap buffer overflow in nft_fwd_dup_netdev_offload in net/netfilter/nf_dup_netdev.c. Local user can escalate to root via crafted nftables expressions.

cves.name: Looney Tunables glibc Buffer Overflow
cves.description: Buffer overflow in GNU C Library's dynamic loader ld.so while processing the GLIBC_TUNABLES environment variable. Allows local privilege escalation to root on default installs.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade glibc to >= 2.39 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: ptrace PTRACE_TRACEME Local Privilege Escalation
cves.description: Kernel ptrace vulnerability in kernel/ptrace.c. A bug in PTRACE_TRACEME allows local users to obtain root privileges by leveraging a parent process with incorrect credentials.

cves.name: Netfilter nft_set_elem_init Type Confusion
cves.description: Type confusion vulnerability in nft_set_elem_init in net/netfilter/nf_tables_api.c. Allows heap buffer overflow for local privilege escalation to root.

cves.name: Netfilter nft_byteorder Stack Buffer Overflow
cves.description: Out-of-bounds read/write in nft_byteorder_eval in net/netfilter/nft_byteorder.c. Local user with CAP_NET_ADMIN can achieve privilege escalation.

cves.name: io_uring Registered Buffer Use-After-Free
cves.description: Use-after-free in io_uring registered buffer management. A local user can exploit the flaw to escalate privileges by manipulating registered buffers.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.7-rc1 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: TIPC Heap Buffer Overflow
cves.description: Heap buffer overflow in net/tipc/crypto.c in the TIPC protocol. An attacker within the network can send specially crafted messages to achieve remote code execution.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 5.15-rc1 or disable TIPC module.

cves.name: Linux Kernel Network Route Use-After-Free
cves.description: Use-after-free in net/ipv4/route.c in the Linux kernel's network route management. Exploited in the wild by Android malware for privilege escalation.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.9.3 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: Linux Kernel USB Video Class Out-of-Bounds Write
cves.description: Out-of-bounds write in the Linux kernel's USB Video Class (UVC) driver when parsing frame types. Actively exploited in Android for privilege escalation.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.12.1 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: Stack Clash PIE Binary Stack Buffer Overflow
cves.description: Linux kernel does not properly restrict stack size for PIE (Position Independent Executable) binaries, allowing local privilege escalation via stack/heap collision.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 4.11.5 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: Binder Use-After-Free (Android/Linux)
cves.description: Use-after-free in the Android Binder driver. The ipc_binder code does not properly manage reference counts, allowing local privilege escalation. Exploited by NSO Group.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 4.14.150 or apply Android security patches.

cves.name: AF_PACKET Socket Memory Corruption
cves.description: Memory corruption in the AF_PACKET socket implementation due to incorrect bounds check in tpacket_rcv. Allows container escape and local root.

cves.name: ESP Transformation Heap Buffer Overflow
cves.description: Heap buffer overflow in net/ipv4/esp4.c and net/ipv6/esp6.c via large messages in IPsec ESP transformation. Local user can overwrite kernel heap objects.

cves.name: perf_event Heap Out-of-Bounds Write
cves.description: Out-of-bounds write in the Linux kernel's perf subsystem. Incorrect inheritance of perf_event_attr.size allows heap buffer overflow, enabling local privilege escalation.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.7-rc4 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: ktls Out-of-Bounds Write in splice
cves.description: Out-of-bounds write in the Linux kernel's TLS subsystem (ktls). When splicing data into a ktls socket, the kernel fails to account for MSG_SENDPAGE_NOTLAST, leading to local privilege escalation.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.7-rc5 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: AF_VSOCK Race Condition Privilege Escalation
cves.description: Race condition in the AF_VSOCK implementation in the Linux kernel before 5.10.13. Multiple race conditions in vsock allow local users to escalate privileges via crafted system calls.

cves.name: tc_flower Use-After-Free Privilege Escalation
cves.description: Use-after-free in the tcindex classifier (net/sched/cls_tcindex.c). A local user can trigger the flaw when deleting a perfect hash filter, enabling local privilege escalation.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 6.2.8 or apply vendor patches.

cves.name: Netfilter nf_tables Stack Out-of-Bounds Access
cves.description: Stack out-of-bounds access in nft_validate_register_store in net/netfilter/nf_tables_api.c. Local user with CAP_NET_ADMIN can write to stack memory for privilege escalation.

cves.name: GameOver(lay) Ubuntu OverlayFS Permission Bypass
cves.description: Local privilege escalation in Ubuntu kernels where OverlayFS does not properly check permissions when setting file capabilities. Variant of CVE-2023-2640.

cves.name: eBPF Verifier Type Confusion
cves.description: The eBPF verifier in the Linux kernel before 5.16.2 has a type confusion vulnerability. A local attacker can exploit the bug for pointer arithmetic, enabling privilege escalation.
cves.recommendation: Upgrade to kernel >= 5.16.2. Restrict unprivileged eBPF.
